yesterdayStoryline.headline: Seven-goal Germany set early Group E marker
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Japan rescued a Group F point, while Ivory Coast and Sweden made opening wins count.

todayStoryline.headline: Groups G and H open across four U.S. venues
todayStoryline.subtitle: Spain and Uruguay begin Group H, while Belgium and Iran carry Group G expectations.

yesterday.scorers: Felix Nmecha 6', Nico Schlotterbeck 38', Kai Havertz 45+5' pen, 88', Jamal Musiala 47', Nathaniel Brown 68', Deniz Undav 78' (GER) · Livano Comenencia 21' (CUR)

lines: Germany finished ruthlessly after Curaçao briefly levelled through a historic first World Cup goal.
The margin put Germany immediately in control of Group E's goal difference picture.

yesterday.scorers: Virgil van Dijk 51', Crysencio Summerville 64' (NED) · Keito Nakamura 57', Daichi Kamada 89' (JPN)

lines: A flat first half gave way to four goals and Japan's late set-piece pressure.
Both sides left Group F with points but clear defensive lessons.
Ecuador struck the bar twice early before Ivory Coast's substitutes shifted the rhythm.
Amad Diallo's 90th-minute winner ended Ecuador's long unbeaten run in Group E.

yesterday.scorers: Yasin Ayari 7', 90+6', Alexander Isak 30', Viktor Gyokeres 59', Mattias Svanberg 84' (SWE) · Omar Rekik 43' (TUN)

lines: Sweden's front two combined fluently, with Ayari adding two long-range finishes.
The 5-1 win leaves Sweden top of Group F after the opening round.

facts.home: Lamine Yamal was still doing school homework during Spain’s Euro 2024 camp.
facts.away: Cape Verde’s Blue Sharks reached their first World Cup with just over 500,000 people.

facts.home: Belgium goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois became an esports-team owner with DUX Gaming.
facts.away: Egypt’s Essam El-Hadary saved a World Cup penalty aged 45 in 2018.

facts.home: Saudi Arabia’s viral 2022 halftime speech asked whether players wanted Messi photos.
facts.away: Uruguay squads famously pack mountains of yerba mate for tournament hotel life.

facts.home: Iran’s Milad Mohammadi once kissed the ball, then abandoned a somersault throw-in.
facts.away: New Zealand exited the 2010 World Cup unbeaten, which still feels backwards.

news.headline: G7 leaders meet in France after U.S.-Iran deal announcement
news.body: Leaders gathered in Évian-les-Bains for talks expected to cover Iran, Ukraine, trade imbalances and critical minerals. The summit opens as allies assess Washington's new Iran framework and seek common ground on security and economic risks.

footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs
